summary: DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp when running a Blazor WASM app upgraded from .NET 9 to .NET 10, affecting both SkiaSharp 3.116.1 and 3.119.2-preview.1. Duplicate of #3422 which describes the same .NET 10 Blazor WASM breakage.

stepsToReproduce: Have a Blazor WASM app using SkiaSharp that works on .NET 9
Run the app and observe DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp

repoLinks.description: Reporter's repo (link from template, not a real repro)

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The WASM native assets are compiled for emscripten 3.1.56 (for net9.0+). .NET 10 may ship with a newer emscripten version that is ABI-incompatible with these .a files. No .NET 10-specific emscripten build exists yet.

regression.reason: Reporter confirms .NET 9 worked with 3.116.1. The upgrade to .NET 10 broke WASM static linking — this is a .NET SDK change, not a SkiaSharp code change.

analysis.summary: Blazor WASM uses static linking (NativeFileReference) to embed libSkiaSharp.a into dotnet.wasm at build time. The MSBuild targets select emscripten 3.1.56 .a files for net9.0+ via VersionGreaterThanOrEquals. .NET 10 likely ships a newer emscripten toolchain that is ABI-incompatible with the 3.1.56-compiled static libraries, causing the link to fail silently and producing a DllNotFoundException at runtime. This is a duplicate of #3422 which reports the identical Blazor WASM + .NET 10 breakage.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug because DllNotFoundException is a clear runtime failure, not a usage question. Area is libSkiaSharp.native because the root cause is WASM native binary compatibility with .NET 10's emscripten toolchain. The community comment correctly identifies this as a duplicate of #3422, which was filed 5 days earlier with more detail (repro code, project file). Both describe the same regression when upgrading to .NET 10.

keySignals.text: Started getting this runtime error after upgrading Blazor app from .Net 9 to .Net 10
keySignals.interpretation: Confirms regression tied to .NET 10 upgrade, not a SkiaSharp version change.
Multiple SkiaSharp versions fail, confirming this is a platform compatibility issue not specific to one release.
Community identifies this as a duplicate of the earlier .NET 10 Blazor WASM issue.

codeInvestigation.finding: NativeFileReference for net9.0+ uses VersionGreaterThanOrEquals(9.0) which matches .NET 10. Selects emscripten 3.1.56 .a files. If .NET 10 ships a newer emscripten, these pre-compiled .a files may fail to link.
TFMNext is empty — SkiaSharp does not yet have a net10.0 TFM target. TFMCurrent is net8.0. The SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor package only targets net6.0/net8.0, relying on .NET forward compatibility.

Blazor csproj references Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web for net6-9 only. No net10.0 reference exists, but the managed assembly should still work via TFM fallback — the issue is in the native linking, not the managed layer.

nextQuestions: What emscripten version does .NET 10 ship with, and is it ABI-compatible with 3.1.56?
Does building SkiaSharp WASM with the .NET 10 emscripten toolchain produce working .a files?
Is a new emscripten version entry needed in the NativeFileReference conditions for .NET 10?

actionability.reason: Same root cause as #3422 — Blazor WASM + .NET 10 produces DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp. #3422 was filed first with more detail (complete project file and repro code). Both issues need the same fix: compiling WASM native assets for .NET 10's emscripten version.

actions.description: Fix platform label from Windows-Classic to WASM — this is a Blazor WASM issue, not a Windows desktop issue
Mark as duplicate of #3422 (identical .NET 10 Blazor WASM breakage)
Acknowledge the report and redirect to #3422
actions.comment:
Thanks for reporting this. This is a duplicate of #3422 — both describe the same issue: SkiaSharp's WASM native assets (compiled for emscripten 3.1.56) don't link correctly with .NET 10's Blazor WASM toolchain.

We're tracking the .NET 10 WASM compatibility fix in #3422. In the meantime, the only workaround is to stay on .NET 9 for Blazor WASM projects using SkiaSharp.
